visualization.description: Bar chart showing distinct student counts per scholarship type.
visualization.title: Number of students by scholarship type

resultRaw.sql:
SELECT s.scholarship_type AS scholarship_type,
       COUNT(DISTINCT s.student_id) AS student_count
FROM public.scholarships AS s
GROUP BY s.scholarship_type
ORDER BY student_count DESC, scholarship_type ASC;

issues: metadata.rowCount is 3 but metadata.sample contains only 2 rows — the sample appears incomplete or truncated.

Chart encodes scholarship_type (nominal) on the x-axis and student_count (quantitative) on the y-axis, which matches the GROUP BY and COUNT(DISTINCT) in the SQL; the chart also sorts categories by descending student_count (informational).

SQL uses COUNT(DISTINCT student_id). If the user instead wanted the total number of scholarship awards (including multiple awards per student), the metric should be COUNT(*) rather than COUNT(DISTINCT student_id).

visualization.description: Bar chart showing distinct student counts per scholarship type.
visualization.title: Number of students by scholarship type

result.sql:
SELECT s.scholarship_type AS scholarship_type,
       COUNT(DISTINCT s.student_id) AS student_count
FROM public.scholarships AS s
GROUP BY s.scholarship_type
ORDER BY student_count DESC, scholarship_type ASC;

issues: metadata.rowCount is 3 but metadata.sample contains only 2 rows — the sample appears incomplete or truncated.

Chart encodes scholarship_type (nominal) on the x-axis and student_count (quantitative) on the y-axis, which matches the GROUP BY and COUNT(DISTINCT) in the SQL; the chart also sorts categories by descending student_count (informational).

SQL uses COUNT(DISTINCT student_id). If the user instead wanted the total number of scholarship awards (including multiple awards per student), the metric should be COUNT(*) rather than COUNT(DISTINCT student_id).
